title: Evacuation Go-Bag
summary: Keep a small, portable bag ready with water, food, light, communications, first aid, hygiene, documents, and basic tools so you can leave home quickly during an evacuation.
quickAnswer: Pack a grab-and-go bag with water, food, flashlight, radio, first aid, medications, hygiene supplies, copies of important documents, cash, and seasonal clothing. Store it near an exit and review it regularly.
whenToUse: For evacuations, sudden disasters (fire, flood, earthquake, storm, conflict), or when you must leave home or work on short notice.
preferredAction: Pack a portable bag with essentials you can carry quickly, following official emergency kit guidance and customizing for your household.
backupAction: If you cannot assemble a full bag, gather the highest-priority items: medications, water, IDs, a flashlight, and a way to contact family.
stepByStepActions: Choose a durable, comfortable bag you can carry at a run — a backpack works best.
Pack non-perishable food and water for at least 72 hours where feasible.
Add a flashlight, batteries or hand-crank light, a radio, phone charger, and a power bank.
Include a first aid kit, personal medications, and basic hygiene items.
Add copies of important documents (IDs, insurance, emergency contacts) and cash in small bills.
Include seasonal clothing layers, protective footwear, an emergency blanket, and a whistle.
Add child, pet, or disability-specific items if needed.
Store the bag near an exit and review or rotate contents at least every six months.
warnings: A bag too heavy to carry quickly may delay evacuation — balance completeness with portability.
Do not delay evacuation waiting to pack more items.
Medications expire and must be rotated regularly.
whatNotToDo: Do not rely on last-minute packing during an evacuation.
Do not store the kit where it can be blocked in an emergency.
redFlags: Evacuation order issued with no bag ready.
Go-bag contents include medications that have expired or are now missing.
If local authorities issue evacuation or shelter-in-place orders, follow them and bring your bag.
preparednessTips: Follow local or national emergency preparedness websites for region-specific recommendations.
Include items for children, elders, and pets as needed.
Practice a quick grab-and-go drill with your household.

sourceReferences.title: Build A Kit

notes: Consolidated from evacuation-go-bag (canonical, scenario layer) and personal-evacuation-go-bag-adult (archived, preparedness layer). Steps merged: 8 total (6 from archive as base structure, enriched with canonical's child/pet/disability and footwear items). Source refs: 5 total (4 from canonical + 1 unique from archive). Archive slug should be set to review_status:archived after this version is approved. Note: expedition-preparation-checklist has alternativeToGuideSlugs referencing the archive slug — update that reference to evacuation-go-bag.

tools.name: Flashlight
tools.description: A battery-powered portable light source for illumination in low-light conditions
tools.context: Essential for navigating in the dark during an evacuation or power outage

tools.name: Power bank
tools.description: A rechargeable battery pack used to charge phones and small electronic devices
tools.context: Keeps phone and communication devices charged when grid power is unavailable

tools.name: Radio
tools.description: A radio used in survival and emergency preparedness
tools.context: Receives emergency broadcasts and evacuation updates when cell networks are down

tools.name: First Aid Kit
tools.description: A first aid kit used in survival and emergency preparedness
tools.context: Provides immediate wound care and medical supplies during evacuation

tools.name: Emergency blanket
tools.description: A lightweight reflective sheet that retains body heat and provides weather protection
tools.context: Provides warmth and weather protection if sheltering outdoors after evacuation

tools.name: Whistle
tools.description: A small device that produces a loud high-pitched sound for signaling and attracting attention
tools.context: Signals for help if trapped or separated from group during evacuation

tools.name: Batteries
tools.description: Portable electrochemical power sources for flashlights, radios, and electronic devices
tools.context: Powers flashlight and radio when rechargeable options are depleted

tools.name: Knife
tools.description: A fixed or folding blade used for cutting, carving, and general utility tasks
tools.context: General utility tool for opening packages, cutting cordage, and improvised repairs

tools.name: Water bottle
tools.description: A sealed container for carrying and storing drinking water
tools.context: Carries drinking water supply for the evacuation period
